name: Here the word fitnah is used in the sense of 'persecution'. It refers to a situation
introduction:
whereby either a person or a group is subjected to harassment and intimidation for having
accepted, as true, a set of ideas contrary to those currently held, and for striving to effect
reforms in the existing order of society by preaching what is good and condemning what is
wrong. Such a situation must be changed, if need be, by the force of arms.
Bloodshed is bad, but when one group of people imposes its ideology and forcibly prevents
others from accepting the truth, then it becomes guilty of an even more serious crime. In
such circumstances, it is perfectly legitimate to remove that oppressive group by the force of
arms.
192 Sn Stab c ail G51 3455 aE
